structural_claim: Geometry and topology fundamentally constrain wave propagation and create qualitatively new phenomena including band gaps, localization, and topologically protected states

supporting_results.structural_conclusion: Spacetime geometry creates causal boundaries (event horizons) that fundamentally constrain propagation of all fields including light
Rotating geometries create frame-dragging effects that alter propagation characteristics in a topology-dependent way
Periodic geometry creates band gaps that forbid wave propagation at certain frequencies
Global topological properties of band structures create protected edge states immune to local perturbations
